executive_summary: During the reporting period November 2025 through April 2026, 22 MAUDE adverse event reports were identified for product code QIH, with 50% classified as AI-related (11 of 22 reports). A potential signal has been identified based on the elevated AI-related rate and two root-cause hypotheses involving algorithmic performance degradation and false-negative classification errors. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 1. Reporting Period Overview
sections.content: This report covers the period from November 1, 2025 through April 30, 2026 for FDA product code QIH. Data sources include 22 adverse event reports sourced from the FDA MAUDE database via the openFDA public API. All events were ingested and analyzed using MaudeSignal v0.1.0. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 2. Adverse Event Summary
sections.content: Of 22 reports, 0 resulted in death, 2 involved serious injury, 18 involved device malfunction, and 2 were classified as other. Event types included malfunctions (82%), injuries (9%), and other (9%). No patient deaths were attributable to device performance during this period. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 3. AI-Related Signal Analysis
sections.content: Eleven of 22 reports (50%) were classified as AI-related by the maude-narrative-extractor skill. The most prevalent failure modes were performance_degradation (7 reports) and false_negative_clinical (4 reports). Average extraction confidence was 0.88. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 4. Root Cause Analysis Summary
sections.content: Two root-cause hypotheses were generated for clusters meeting the minimum size threshold. The performance_degradation cluster (n=7) yielded a hypothesis of distributional shift in input data quality with confidence 0.82. The false_negative_clinical cluster (n=4) yielded a hypothesis of threshold calibration drift with confidence 0.71. Both hypotheses require further investigation. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 5. Trend Analysis
sections.content: Trend analysis over the 90-day window identified a moderate increasing trend in AI-related failure rate (Mann-Kendall tau=0.45, p=0.03), classified as an elevated signal. The report volume showed no statistically significant trend. Monitoring should continue at the current cadence. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 6. Literature Review
sections.content: 3 relevant PubMed publications and 2 ClinicalTrials.gov studies were identified through automated search. These sources provide context for the observed failure modes but do not constitute primary evidence for this report. Full citation details are available in the MaudeSignal database. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 7. Signal Assessment and Conclusions
sections.content: Based on the 50% AI-related rate, two root-cause hypotheses, and a statistically significant increasing trend, a potential signal has been identified for product code QIH. The signal does not yet meet confirmed status as no causal mechanism has been independently verified. Continued monitoring and CAPA initiation are recommended. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

sections.title: 8. Recommended Actions
sections.content: Four recommended actions have been identified based on this analysis. First, continue monthly MAUDE signal monitoring. Second, initiate CAPA investigation for performance_degradation cluster. Third, notify device manufacturer of potential signal per 21 CFR 803. Fourth, schedule 90-day re-analysis to assess trend trajectory. DRAFT — REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW BEFORE SUBMISSION.

recommended_actions: Continue monthly MAUDE signal monitoring for product code QIH.
Initiate CAPA investigation for the performance_degradation cluster (n=7, confidence 0.82).
Notify device manufacturer of potential signal per applicable reporting requirements.
Schedule 90-day re-analysis to assess whether trend escalates to confirmed signal.
